#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# monitor_cpu.sh — CPU Usage Monitor
# Samples CPU usage every 5 seconds for 1 minute (12 samples).
# Output is printed to stdout AND saved to /tmp/mcp-workspace/cpu_monitor.log
# =============================================================================

LOG_FILE="/tmp/mcp-workspace/cpu_monitor.log"
SAMPLES=12
INTERVAL=5

# ── Helper: get CPU usage % via /proc/stat (two-snapshot delta) ──────────────
get_cpu_usage() {
    # Snapshot 1
    read -r _ u1 n1 s1 id1 iw1 _ < /proc/stat
    sleep 0.25
    # Snapshot 2
    read -r _ u2 n2 s2 id2 iw2 _ < /proc/stat

    idle1=$((id1 + iw1))
    idle2=$((id2 + iw2))
    total1=$((u1 + n1 + s1 + id1 + iw1))
    total2=$((u2 + n2 + s2 + id2 + iw2))

    delta_idle=$(( idle2  - idle1  ))
    delta_total=$(( total2 - total1 ))

    if [ "$delta_total" -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "0.0"
    else
        awk "BEGIN { printf \"%.1f\", (1 - $delta_idle / $delta_total) * 100 }"
    fi
}

# ── Ensure log directory exists ───────────────────────────────────────────────
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$LOG_FILE")"

# ── Header ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
HEADER="=== CPU Monitoring Started at $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') ==="
echo "$HEADER" | tee "$LOG_FILE"

# ── Sampling loop ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
for (( i=1; i<=SAMPLES; i++ )); do
    LOOP_START=$(date +%s%N)          # nanoseconds for precise sleep correction

    TIMESTAMP=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    CPU_USAGE=$(get_cpu_usage)
    LINE="[${TIMESTAMP}] CPU: ${CPU_USAGE}%"

    echo "$LINE" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"

    # Sleep the remainder of the interval (accounting for measurement time ~0.25s)
    if [ "$i" -lt "$SAMPLES" ]; then
        LOOP_END=$(date +%s%N)
        ELAPSED_MS=$(( (LOOP_END - LOOP_START) / 1000000 ))
        SLEEP_MS=$(( INTERVAL * 1000 - ELAPSED_MS ))
        [ "$SLEEP_MS" -gt 0 ] && sleep "$(awk "BE

#!/usr/bin/env node
/**
 * AI Sandbox HTTP wrapper for the Test 7 batch runner.
 *
 * Listens on port 3000 (ALB requirement) and satisfies the health check at
 * GET / while providing a simple API to trigger and monitor batch runs.
 *
 * Routes:
 *   GET  /        — HTML status page; always returns 200 (ALB health check)
 *   GET  /status  — JSON run state
 *   GET  /logs    — plain-text log tail  (?n=N lines, default 100)
 *   POST /run     — start a run; body is JSON with optional overrides:
 *                     { models, scenarios, defences, reps,
 *                       s3Bucket, s3Prefix, s3Region, awsRegion, runId }
 *                   Returns 202 if accepted, 409 if a run is already in progress.
 */

import http from "http";
import { spawn } from "child_process";
import { readFileSync } from "fs";
